question: Is the general scene (worker, wheelbarrow, scaffolding, smartphone) rendered in a realistic photographic style?
Are the bricks in the wheelbarrow rendered in a voxel art style (chunky, cubic geometry)?
Is the bottom of the voxel-style brick stack visibly obscured by the edge of the realistic wheelbarrow?
Is the distant tower crane rendered in a copperplate etching line-drawing style?
Is the copperplate etching style crane segmentally obscured by realistic scaffolding poles?
Is there a neon wireframe holographic safety boundary floating above the danger zone?
Does the worker's realistic arm visually pierce through the neon wireframe boundary lines to create depth?
Is the dust cloud rendered in a traditional Chinese ink wash freehand style?
Are the ink wash dust strokes semi-transparent, overlaying the realistic background?
Do the ink wash dust strokes obscure the worker's lower body?
Does the smartphone screen display a construction blueprint in an isometric vector style?
Is one corner of the vector-style screen content obscured by the worker's realistic gloved thumb?
Does the copperplate etching crane exhibit fine, engraved-like line work or crosshatching?
Does the ink wash dust cloud exhibit brush-like texture and diffusion typical of Chinese painting?
Does the neon wireframe boundary exhibit glowing or luminous qualities?
